coding_rules.rule: Restrict all code to very simple control flow constructs. Do not use direct or indirect recursion.

All loops must have a fixed upper-bound. It must be trivially possible for a checking tool to prove statically that a preset upper-bound on the number of iterations cannot be exceeded. If the loop-bound cannot be proven statically, the rule is considered violated.

No function should be longer than what can be printed on a single sheet of paper in a standard reference format with one line per statement and one line per declaration. Typically no more than about 60 lines of code per function.
Data objects must be declared at the smallest possible level of scope.
All code must be compiled from the first day of development with all compiler warnings enabled at the compiler’s most pedantic setting. All code must compile with these settings without any warnings.

ui_rules.rule: Instead of using values like border-white/[0.06] or bg-white/[0.03], use values like border-white/6 or bg-white/3 to maintain consistency and readability in the codebase and also to ensure we comply with latest Tailwind CSS syntax.

Use descriptive titles for interactive elements like buttons and selects to enhance accessibility. For example, add title attributes such as title='Expand' to buttons that toggle visibility or expand sections, providing clear context for screen readers and improving overall user experience.

Instead of using values like min-w-[180px] or h-[300px], use values like min-w-45 or h-75 (their equivalent in Tailwind CSS) to maintain consistency and readability in the codebase and also to ensure we comply with latest Tailwind CSS syntax.

SEO is topmost priority. Ensure that all interactive elements and important content are accessible and properly structured for search engines. Use semantic HTML tags, descriptive alt attributes for images, and ensure that the website is easily navigable for both users and search engines.

Instead of using values like bg-gradient-to-[xyz], use values like bg-linear-to-[xyz] to maintain consistency and readability in the codebase and also to ensure we comply with latest Tailwind CSS syntax.

Design should not look ai generated. It should be artistic, emotional, and human. Avoid using design elements that are commonly associated with AI-generated content, such as gradients, pills, and other overused design trends. Instead, focus on creating a unique and authentic design that resonates with the target audience and reflects the brand's identity.

Do not use generic or ai generated common text for the website. All text content should be original, engaging, and tailored to the brand's voice and target audience. Avoid using placeholder text or commonly used phrases that may come across as generic or AI-generated. Instead, focus on crafting compelling and meaningful content that effectively communicates the brand's message and values.
